title: Segment-specialist rank ensemble
summary: Dry slick races, wet/intermediate phases, fresh-post-pit rows, late mandatory-stop rows, and pre-season testing likely follow different mechanisms.
rationale: Dry slick races, wet/intermediate phases, fresh-post-pit rows, late mandatory-stop rows, and pre-season testing likely follow different mechanisms. A single AutoGluon model may underfit minority regimes even if its overall CV is strong.
implementation_hint: Train several small specialist models or feature subsets for dry-slick, wet-weather, early/mid/late race, PitStop==1/fresh-tyre, and high-stop-debt rows. Blend OOF predictions by segment gates using rank-normalized probabilities rather than raw calibration, and keep a strong global model as the fallback.
expected_effect: Expected to gain on rare but high-signal regimes without disturbing the already strong global rank ordering.
risk: Specialists can overfit small segments; require OOF blending and avoid segment definitions that use target-derived future information.
